note: User review caught the design flaw: keyed 'description is not a trigger' on user-invocable:false OR metadata.internal, but per Claude Code docs those do NOT suppress model auto-invocation (disable-model-invocation:true does, and it would break by-name loading these skills require). Corrected framing: these are agent-invoked-by-name callee skills (resolved via universal-plugin.json + artifact-type); description is identity-for-caller, kept minimal + non-trigger-shaped to minimize spurious harness matching.

Ratified freeze re-open (by:unional in-session). Redesign: detection=user-invocable:false only (drop metadata.internal, 17/41, a visibility flag; verified 0 false-positives vs gateways); Q3 sharpened to require 'Internal skill: by name only' prefix; add inverse-Q1 (flag user-facing trigger language on a by-name callee); keep Q17 op-detail guard; fix prose justification. Companion: migrate 41 shipped descriptions to the sharpened prefix (same PR).

Ratified re-open #2 (by:unional): name the category. 'internal' is overloaded — metadata.internal:true is the DISTINCT 'project-local internal' category (repo/contributor tooling in .agents/skills), correctly described by skill-design.md. This mission's category is different: SHIPPED plugin sub-skills that are decomposed, loaded/invoked-by-name reusable PARTS of a larger capability. Named 'Partial Skill'. Verified via Claude Code docs: 'Internal skill:' is NOT a harness convention (safe to change); harness has no metadata field and no first-class 'skill-as-a-part' concept. Rename: category term -> 'Partial Skill'; description prefix -> 'Partial Skill:' (migrate to full form 'Partial Skill: invoke by name only — ...'); engine isInternalSkill -> isPartialSkill; Q3 requires the 'Partial Skill:' lead. Fold into PR #144.
